original: Battle of Hogwarts: Fictional battle that ended the Second Wizarding War with the death of Lord Voldemort at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry
Hafþór Júlíus Björnsson, the Icelandic actor who played Ser Gregor "The Mountain" Clegane in Game of Thrones, sets a world deadlifting record by lifting 501kg (1,104lb)
First non-invasive mind reader developed by researchers at the University of Texas at Austin by combing functional magnetic resonance imaging with AI (results hit and miss so far)
Drone delivers a kidney for transplant surgery in Baltimore, Maryland, for the first time
First film in theMarvelCinematic Universe released: "Iron Man" directed byJon Favreau, starringRobert Downey Jr. as Tony Stark
James Dyson, British inventor, industrial designer, and entrepreneur (bagless vacuum cleaner; bladeless fan), born in Cromer, Norfolk, England
New research shows plants "talk" to each other through their roots and the soil in a study on corn published in the journal "PLOS ONE"
Leicester City wins the English Premier League title after starting the season at 5,000-1 odds
Barcelona football playerLionel Messibreaks the European goal-scoring record with 68 goals
Date for first humans or hominins in the Philippines pushed back ten-fold after report of site on Luzon with butchered rhinoceros 709,000 years ago
PresidentBill Clintonannounces that accurate GPS access would no longer be restricted to the United States military
Sergei Prokofiev's musical "Peter and the Wolf" premieres in Moscow
A clean-up on Mt Everest has removed three metric tons (6,613 pounds) of rubbish and four bodies in just two weeks
Second-only sketch known ofLeonardo da Vincifrom Queen Elizabeth's collection revealed on the 500th anniversary of the artist's death
Ted [Samuel Frederick] Dabney, American engineer, founder of Atari (Pong), born in San Francisco, California (d. 2018)
MLB St. Louis CardinalStan Musialhits 5 HRs in a doubleheader against the New York Giants at Busch Stadium
New York YankeeLou Gehrigends 2,130 consecutive game streak when he sits out as the Yankees beat the Tigers 22-2 at Briggs Stadium in Detroit, Michigan
Hannibal Goodwin patents celluloid photographic film (used inThomas Edison's Kinetoscope)
A pastel version of "The Scream" by Norwegian painterEdvard Munchsells at auction for $119,922,500
Children's crusade begins in Birmingham, Alabama. More than 600 African American school children arrested for marching against segregation, organised byJames Beveland the SCLC
Hungary's reform government led by Miklós Németh cuts a hole in the electric wire fence between it and Austria beginning the fall of the Iron Curtain
Cincinnati's Fred Toney and Chicago's Hippo Vaughn pitch a dual no-hitter. Vaughn gives up 2 hits and a run in the 10th, letting Cincinnati win 1-0
-May 10] Vivekananda (Sri Lanka) begins nonstop ride, cycling 187 hrs, 28 min, around Vihara Maha Devi Park, Colombo, Sri Lanka
Floyd Mayweather Jr.beatsManny Pacquiaoon points in 12 rounds in world welterweight unification fight, shatters all financial records for a boxing match
"Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery", starringMike MyersandElizabeth Hurley, is released
Jack Norworth and Albert Von Tilzer register their popular song "Take Me Out to the Ball Game" for copyright
William Herscheldiscovers the first binary star, Xi Ursae Majoris
First ascent of Shishapangma the fourteenth highest mountain in the world and lowest of the Eight-thousanders
First baseball game of the Negro National League is played in Indianapolis
John Cage's "Water Music"  - for a solo pianist, using a piano, a radio, whistles, water containers, and a deck of cards - premieres in New York City
US Lab detects high-temperature microwave radiation from Venus
French newspapers publish lists ofJules Verne's unpublished work
Athanasius Kircher, German Jesuit priest and inventor (magic lantern), born in Abbacy of Fulda, Germany (d. 1680)
Pink Floyd's "Another Brick in Wall (Part II)" is banned in South Africa
Joel Embiidwins the NBA Most Valuable Player Award
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON:Terry Sawchukmakes 40 saves as Toronto Maple Leafs beat Montreal Canadiens, 3-1 in Game 6 to take title, 4-2
MLB Washington SenatorWalter Johnsonpitches his 100th shutout, beats Yanks 3-0
MLB baseball playerTed Williams' batting average hits lowest point of the season at .308; finishes over .400
American singerElla Fitzgeraldrecords "A-Tisket, A-Tasket" with Chick Webb and His Orchestra
1978 NFL Draft: Earl Campbell from University of Texas first pick by Houston Oilers
Early Bird satellite goes into commercial service
First scheduled jet airliner passenger service begins with a British Overseas Airways Corporation (BOAC) Comet from London to Johannesburg, carrying 36 passengers
Nannie Helen Burroughs, American educator and civil rights activist (founded National Training School for Women and Girls), born in Orange, Virginia (d. 1961)
Satyajit Ray, Indian director (Goddess, Adversary), born in Calcutta, India (d. 1992)
Pinky Lee, American children's show host (Pinky Lee Show), born in Saint Paul, Minnesota (d. 1993)
Mari Natsuki [Junko Nakajima], Japanese singer, dancer, and stage and screen actress (Spirited Away), born in Tokyo, Japan
(Fred Lincoln) "Link" Wray, Native American rock guitarist ("Rumble"), born in Dunn, North Carolina (d. 2005)
Tony Asher, English-American lyricist ("God Only Knows"; "Wouldn't It Be Nice"; "That's Life"), born in London, England
Lou Gramm [Grammatico], American rock singer-songwriter (Foreigner - "Hot Blooded"; "Cold As Ice"), born in Rochester, New York
Israeli television begins transmitting
New Zealand reopens its borders to international visitors from more than 60 countries after being closed for two years during the pandemic
John Elwayannounces his retirement from the NFL
The Beatles' "The Beatles' Second Album" goes #1 and stays #1 for 5 weeks
Pulitzer Prize for Literature awarded to novelistPearl S. Buckfor "The Good Earth", the first American woman to win
Pulitzer Prize for Drama is awarded toThornton Wilderfor his play "Our Town"
Transportation Expo 86 opens in Vancouver, BC
British liner Queen Elizabeth II leaves Southampton on its maiden voyage to NY
Peruvian defenders fight off Spanish fleet at the Battle of Callao
The Folies Trévise (later cabaret hall Folies Bergère) opens as an opera house in Paris, France
Territory of Oklahoma created - exists until 1907
